hero_product_name: the AnyWell YS16-1 PLUS electric wheelchair from the white-background reference photos
campaign_goal: Create one new universal 9:16 short ad for the AnyWell YS16-1 PLUS, emotionally inspired by getting back outdoors near home without recreating any real customer story.
target_audience: American seniors who need mobility support, their spouses, and adult children buying for loved ones.
core_selling_points:
- smooth stable powered ride
- wide supportive seat
- calm starts near home
- easy everyday outdoor freedom
use_scenarios:
- back doorway or covered patio threshold
- narrow garden path beside the home
- quiet sidewalk just beyond the yard
visual_style: Realistic, warm, portrait-first American lifestyle advertising with route continuity, anonymous faces, and clear scene progression.
style_tone: warm, calm, dignified, practical, reassuring, never clinical
consistency_anchor: Match the real YS16-1 PLUS wheelchair from the white-background product photos while keeping it only in normal open riding position. Preserve the right-side joystick, wide seat feel, frame, footrest, wheel layout, and real product silhouette. Keep the rear/top-back area compact and proportional to the real chair: no extra rods, poles, antenna-like parts, cane-like extensions, or exaggerated push bars behind the backrest. In every shot, keep the rider and wheelchair proportion close to c1 and c2: the rider naturally fills the broad seat, the chair reads substantial and premium, and neither subject looks oversized or undersized relative to the other.
additional_info: Use Jean's uploaded clip only as emotional direction for quiet freedom outdoors near home. Do not recreate any real person, real house, unfinished ramp, exact woods layout, or testimonial behavior. Keep the same white-haired American senior husband and the same wife across all shots. Faces must remain soft and anonymous, never crisp portrait close-ups. Avoid direct eye contact to camera, avoid face-led hero shots, and avoid anything that reads like a testimonial portrait. Keep actions simple and safe. Leave the generated footage itself free of in-scene captions, logos, prices, or UI.
music: Warm restrained cinematic underscore with light piano and a subtle modern pulse.

concepts.title: Doorway To Garden Path
concepts.core_theme: One believable outing that starts at home, moves through the garden, and opens into a calmer path beyond the yard.
concepts.main_theme: Fresh air should still feel close.
concepts.hook: Fresh air should still feel close.
concepts.cta: See how AnyWell helps bring the outdoors back within reach.
concepts.visual_style: Soft morning light, quiet home edge, enclosed garden middle, open arrival finish.
concepts.continuity_rider_anchor: Keep the same heavy-set white-haired American senior husband age 60+ and the same wife across all three shots. He should visibly fill the wide seat naturally, with chair scale and framing similar to c1 and c2. Keep wardrobe and route direction consistent.
concepts.use_scenarios:
- back doorway or covered patio threshold
- narrow garden path with shrubs close to camera
- quiet sidewalk just beyond a small garden gate
concepts.additional_info: This concept must feel like one continuous route with obvious scene differences. Scene 1 is the start point at the doorway. Scene 2 is clearly deeper in the yard with closer greenery and less house. Scene 3 is clearly more open, with more sky and sidewalk cues beyond the yard. Keep left-to-right screen direction across all three shots.

shots.scene_description: A heavy-set white-haired American senior husband in his 60s calmly drives the YS16-1 PLUS from a bright back doorway or covered patio threshold into the yard while his wife follows with a relieved smile. This must read as the start of one continuous route: the doorway, threshold, and first movement away from the house should be clearly visible. Frame like c1 and c2 with the rider and chair large in portrait, but keep the faces soft and anonymous, more like side profiles than portrait close-ups. Keep the motion left to right and keep the generated footage itself free of in-scene ad text or UI.
shots.camera_movement: smooth front-side push that begins nearly still at the threshold and eases outward into the yard, keeping left-to-right motion
shots.lighting: soft morning daylight with warm highlights and natural depth
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait medium-wide composition, rider and chair large in frame, visible back door or patio threshold, generic North American home details only, no exact source-house cues
shots.transition_anchor: end with the chair rolling fully off the threshold and toward the garden walk, still moving left to right
shots.voiceover_en: Fresh air should still feel close, right from the back door.
shots.key_message: The outing begins right at home.
shots.sfx: soft wheel roll, birds, and light morning ambience

shots.scene_description: The same husband now glides farther from the house on a narrow garden path bordered by shrubs, flowers, or lawn edges, with his right hand clearly on the joystick while his wife walks one step behind him. This must feel like the middle section of the same route, not a repeated doorway shot: greenery should sit closer to camera, the house should feel farther back, and the path should feel more enclosed than scene one. Keep c1 and c2 proportion logic so the rider visibly fills the wide seat naturally and the chair remains large and balanced in frame. Maintain left-to-right screen direction and keep the generated footage itself free of in-scene ad text or UI.
shots.camera_movement: steady joystick-side front-profile tracking shot with subtle forward drift and tight garden parallax
shots.lighting: clean daylight filtered through trees with premium but realistic contrast
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait medium shot with rider and chair taking most of the frame height, shrubs or flowers close to camera, spouse softly visible, the home only distant or partial
shots.transition_anchor: end with the chair approaching a small gate opening or sidewalk break at the edge of the yard, still moving left to right
shots.voiceover_en: The wide seat and smooth ride make the garden path feel easy again.
shots.key_message: Comfort and control are visible in motion.
shots.sfx: smooth motor ambience, leaves, and light outdoor air

shots.scene_description: The same couple emerge onto a quiet sidewalk just beyond the garden gate, creating a clear final step from home to open air. This must read as a new destination: more sky, more open space, less dense greenery, and a calmer wider feeling than the previous shot. Keep the couple lower in frame with soft side profiles or half profiles looking outward rather than into camera. Preserve left-to-right route logic and reserve clean upper-frame space while the generated footage itself stays free of in-scene ad text or UI.
shots.camera_movement: gentle moving settle from the sidewalk approach into a calm hero frame without becoming a portrait close-up
shots.lighting: warm flattering daylight with a polished but realistic finish
shots.composition_and_set_dressing: portrait hero composition on a quiet sidewalk or lane, rider and chair prominent, more sky and open space visible, fewer nearby plants than scene two
shots.transition_anchor: end on a calm confident hold for the close
shots.voiceover_en: By the time the path opens up, freedom feels calm and familiar.
shots.key_message: The route closes on quiet confidence.
shots.sfx: music lift and soft outdoor ambience
